skill.name: diagnosing-bugs
skill.description: Diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Use when the user says "diagnose"/"debug this", or reports something broken/throwing/failing/slow.
skill.summary: Guide agents through a structured debugging loop for hard bugs and performance regressions.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography, command execution, a hardcoded URL, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found the high and medium alerts are false positives from Markdown prose and inline code formatting, while the local debugging script and command-oriented guidance create only low operational risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert In Prose
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts at SKILL.md lines 3 and 68 do not reference cryptographic algorithms or implementation code. They appear to match text fragments inside ordinary debugging guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are descriptive Markdown text about diagnosis and reproducing the user's failure. No cryptographic API, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Are Not Code Execution
low_findings.description: The Ruby or shell backtick alerts in SKILL.md are inline Markdown examples and file references. They do not execute commands by themselves, and SKILL.md contains no executable Ruby or shell backtick expression.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is documentation, and the backticks delimit terms such as file names, command names, and debug tags. No dynamic command construction or untrusted command execution is shown.

low_findings.title: Low Risk: Local Human-In-The-Loop Template
low_findings.description: The script asks a user to open http://localhost:3000 and answer whether an export button failed. This is local debugging guidance, not external network access or system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL targets localhost and the script only prompts for manual UI observations. It does not collect host details, send data to a remote endpoint, or inspect the operating system.

content.user_title: Diagnose Bugs with a Structured Loop
content.value_statement: Hard bugs often take too long because teams start with guesses instead of a reliable signal. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a reproducible loop, ranked hypotheses, focused instrumentation, and regression tests.
actual_capabilities: Builds a red-capable feedback loop before forming debugging theories.
Guides reproduction and minimization of failing scenarios.
Requires ranked, falsifiable hypotheses before instrumentation.
Recommends targeted probes for functional bugs and performance regressions.
Connects fixes to regression tests or documents missing test seams.
Includes a human-in-the-loop shell template for manual reproduction steps.
limitations: Requires access to code, logs, tests, or a reproducible environment.
Cannot guarantee a fix when the failure cannot be reproduced.
May need user input for manual UI steps or production-only failures.
Does not replace domain knowledge for ranking likely causes.

use_cases.title: Debug a Production Regression
use_cases.description: Create a local or replayable signal for a recently introduced failure, then isolate the smallest scenario that still fails.

use_cases.title: Stabilize a Flaky UI Bug
use_cases.description: Raise the reproduction rate with browser automation or guided manual steps before changing application code.

use_cases.title: Investigate a Slow Path
use_cases.description: Measure a baseline, compare versions or configurations, and focus instrumentation on the suspected performance boundary.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Diagnosis
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a visible failure.
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this bug using the diagnosing-bugs workflow. First create a tight feedback loop that can reproduce my exact symptom, then tell me the command or manual loop you used.

prompt_templates.title: Minimize a Reproduction
prompt_templates.scenario: A failure can already be reproduced.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the diagnosing-bugs process to shrink this reproduction. Keep removing inputs, steps, and dependencies one at a time until every remaining part is required for the failure.

prompt_templates.title: Rank Hypotheses
prompt_templates.scenario: The team has several possible causes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate three to five falsifiable hypotheses for this bug. Rank them by likelihood, state the prediction each one makes, and identify the smallest probe that would confirm or reject each one.

prompt_templates.title: Handle a Performance Regression
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this performance regression. Establish a baseline measurement, compare the old and new behavior, design focused probes, and avoid broad logging unless it directly tests a hypothesis.

output_examples.input: The export button fails for some users after the latest release.
output_examples.output: The agent defines a reproduction loop, captures the exact user-facing error, minimizes the required account state, lists ranked causes, and verifies the fix with a regression check.

output_examples.input: A report endpoint became much slower this week.
output_examples.output: The agent records a baseline, compares recent versions, tests likely bottlenecks one at a time, and confirms the final fix against the original slow scenario.

output_examples.input: The bug only happens after several manual UI steps.
output_examples.output: The agent uses the human-in-the-loop template to structure the manual steps, capture observations, and turn the result into a tighter automated or semi-automated loop.

best_practices: Start by building a feedback loop that can fail on the exact symptom.
Minimize the reproduction before testing implementation theories.
Remove temporary instrumentation and document the confirmed cause before finishing.
anti_patterns: Do not start with a single untested theory.
Do not use broad logs when a focused probe can test the prediction.
Do not claim success without rerunning the original reproduction loop.

faq.question: What kind of bugs is this skill for?
faq.answer: It is best for hard functional bugs, flaky failures, and performance regressions that need disciplined reproduction and verification.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: It guides the agent to create and run appropriate local checks when the user allows that work in the current project.

faq.question: Can it handle bugs that only happen in production?
faq.answer: Yes, but it needs a captured artifact, controlled access, or permission to add temporary instrumentation.

faq.question: Why does it require a feedback loop first?
faq.answer: A reliable loop proves the bug exists, makes hypotheses testable, and verifies that the final fix addresses the real symptom.

faq.question: Does it write regression tests?
faq.answer: It asks for a regression test when a correct test seam exists. If no seam exists, it documents that limitation.

faq.question: Can non-engineers use it?
faq.answer: Non-engineers can use it for structured reports, but code access and engineering judgment are usually needed for fixes.
